title: Rich Savory Argentinian White Beans Stew
description: A hearty and savory stew featuring dried white beans, perfect for a lazy Sunday, this keto-friendly dish is a staple of Argentinian cuisine. It is light and flavorful, making it a great option for a comforting meal.

ingredients.name: dried white beans
ingredients.notes: such as cannellini or navy beans

instructions: Rinse the dried white beans and soak them in water for at least 8 hours or overnight.
Drain and rinse the soaked beans, then set them aside.
Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1 minute.
Add the ground beef and cook until it is browned, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Add the diced tomatoes, beef broth, salt, and black pepper to the pot, stirring to combine.
Add the soaked and rinsed white beans to the pot and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer the stew for 1 hour, or until the beans are tender.
Cook ground beef to an internal temperature of 71°C / 160°F (no longer pink in the middle) before serving.
